title: iOS Best Practices – Professional Engineering Standards

meta.title: iOS Development Best Practices – Architecture, Security & Clean Code (2026)
meta.description: Master the professional standards for iOS development. Learn about Protocol-Oriented Programming, Dependency Injection, and thread safety in native Swift.

content.value: Professional iOS development has shifted away from the 'Massive View Controller' era. Today, a Lead Developer focuses on **Composition over Inheritance** and **Value Types over Reference Types**. For your Revochamp platform, these practices ensure that the native iOS code is modular enough to be tested in isolation from the Flutter UI.

In Swift, Protocols are the primary way to define interfaces. Unlike Java or Dart interfaces, Swift protocols can have 'Default Implementations' via Extensions. This allows you to create highly reusable traits (like `Searchable` or `Generatable`) that can be applied to both Structs and Classes.

Avoid hard-coding dependencies inside your View Controllers or Managers. Use **Constructor Injection** to pass services in. This is vital for an Engineering Manager because it makes your native code **testable**—you can easily swap a real AI network service for a 'Mock' version during Unit Testing.

With Swift 6's strict concurrency checks, managing threads is no longer optional. Always mark your ViewModels or UI-updating classes with `@MainActor`. This guarantees that any code modifying the UI will execute on the Main Thread, preventing the most common cause of native iOS crashes.

For a platform like Revochamp, security is paramount. Never store API keys, tokens, or PII (Personally Identifiable Information) in `UserDefaults` or `Info.plist`. Use the **Keychain** for tokens and **Secure Enclave** for biometric-linked keys. Additionally, ensure 'App Transport Security' (ATS) is never disabled in production.

faq.question: Should I use Singletons in iOS?
faq.answer: Use them sparingly. While Apple uses them (e.g., 'URLSession.shared'), global state makes testing difficult. If you use a Singleton, ensure it conforms to a Protocol so you can still inject a mock version during tests.

faq.question: What is the 'Clean Architecture' approach for iOS?
faq.answer: It involves separating the app into Layers: Domain (Entities/Use Cases), Presentation (SwiftUI/VM), and Data (Repositories/API). This mirrors the Clean Architecture patterns you use in Flutter, making the whole system easier to reason about.

faq.question: How should I handle Error Messages for users?
faq.answer: Architects recommend using 'Enums' that conform to the 'LocalizedError' protocol. This allows you to define user-friendly error strings in one place, which can then be easily displayed in the UI or passed back through a Method Channel.

quiz.question: Which programming paradigm is central to Swift's design, favoring composition over deep inheritance?
What is the primary architectural benefit of Dependency Injection?
options: It makes the app run faster
It makes classes easier to test by allowing mock objects
It reduces the size of the app binary
It automatically saves data to the cloud

quiz.question: Which attribute ensures that a class or method always runs on the main UI thread?
